epigraph.text:
The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing. He makes me 
lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters.

content.text:
Division is the inverse operation of multiplication. The rules for 
dividing integers follow the same sign patterns as multiplication.
The Relationship Between Division and Multiplication

Every division problem can be written as a multiplication:
\[
a \div b = c \quad \text{means} \quad b \times c = a
\]

This relationship tells us how to handle signs in division. If 
$(-12) \div 4 = ?$, we need to find what number times 4 gives $-12$. 
Since $4 \times (-3) = -12$, we have $(-12) \div 4 = -3$.

content.title: Dividing Integers
content.content:
- **Same signs** (both positive or both negative): 
 The quotient is **positive**
 $$
 (+) \div (+) &= (+) \\
 (-) \div (-) &= (+)
 $$

- **Different signs** (one positive, one negative): 
 The quotient is **negative**
 $$
 (+) \div (-) &= (-) \\
 (-) \div (+) &= (-)
 $$
These are the same rules as multiplication!

content.title: Basic division
content.solution:
1. $36 \div 9 = 4$ (positive $\div$ positive = positive)

2. $(-42) \div 7 = -6$ (different signs = negative)

3. $56 \div (-8) = -7$ (different signs = negative)

4. $(-72) \div (-9) = 8$ (same signs = positive)

content.title: Division and Zero
content.content:
- **Zero divided by any nonzero number is zero**: 
 $0 \div a = 0$ (when $a \neq 0$)

- **Division by zero is undefined**: 
 $a \div 0$ is undefined (for any $a$)

content.text:
Why is division by zero undefined? Consider $8 \div 0 = ?$. This would 
mean $0 \times ? = 8$. But any number times zero is zero, so there is 
no number that works. Division by zero has no answer—it is undefined.

Division can be written using the fraction bar:
\[
a \div b = \frac{a}{b}
\]

When a fraction has negative numbers, the negative sign can be 
placed in three equivalent positions:
\[
\frac{-a}{b} = \frac{a}{-b} = -\frac{a}{b}
\]

content.title: Equivalent forms
content.problem: Write $\displaystyle \frac{-15}{5}$ in two other equivalent forms.

content.title: Mixed operations
content.solution:
First, compute the numerator:
\[
(-4) \times (-6) = 24
\]
Then divide:
\[
\frac{24}{-8} = -3
\]

content.title: Order of operations
content.solution:
Following order of operations (multiply and divide before add):
$$
(-20) \div 4 + 3 \times (-2) &= -5 + (-6) \\
&= -11
$$

content.title: Division by Zero in Computing
content.content:
When computers attempt to divide by zero, different systems handle it 
differently. Some produce an error message and stop. Others produce 
special values like "infinity" or "NaN" (Not a Number). Early 
computers and calculators sometimes crashed entirely when division by 
zero was attempted. Modern programming languages include safeguards, 
but division by zero remains a common source of software bugs.

marginNotes.text:
Trust in the Lord with all your heart and 
lean not on your own understanding.

As the heavens are higher than the earth, 
so are my ways higher than your ways and my thoughts than your thoughts.

marginNotes.author: Pierre-Simon Laplace
marginNotes.text:
The theory of probabilities is at 
bottom nothing but common sense reduced to calculus.

marginNotes.author: Georg Cantor
marginNotes.text:
In mathematics the art of proposing a 
question must be held of higher value than solving it.
